dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7998 IH: Building Reentry and Inclusive Development for Greater Employment Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Bell introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Ways and Means

form.official-title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to extend and expand the work opportunity tax credit.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Building Reentry and Inclusive Development for Greater Employment Act or the BRIDGE Act.

section.header: Extension and expansion of work opportunity tax credit

subsection.text: Section 51(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by striking December 31, 2025 and inserting December 31, 2030.

paragraph.text: Section 51(d)(4) of such Code is amended to read as follows:
The term qualified criminal justice-impacted individual means any individual who is certified by the designated local agency—

clause.text: convicted of a felony under any statute of the United States or any State, or
incarcerated in any Federal, State, or local correctional institution, or placed on probation, for a period of at least 90 days, and

subparagraph.text: as having a hiring date which is not more than 3 years after—

clause.text: in the case of an individual to whom only clause (i) of subparagraph (A) applies, the last date on which such individual was so convicted or was released from prison,

in the case of an individual to whom only clause (ii) of subparagraph (A) applies, the last date on which such individual was released from such incarceration or was discharged from such probation, and
in the case of an individual to whom both clauses (i) and (ii) of subparagraph (A) apply, the later of the dates specified in clauses (i) and (ii) of this subparagraph.

paragraph.text: Section 51(d) of such Code is amended by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

The term qualified opportunity youth means any individual who is certified by the designated local agency as being an out-of-school youth (as defined in section 129(a)(1)(B) of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act).

subsection.text: Section 51(d)(1) of such Code is amended—

paragraph.text: in subparagraph (C), by striking qualified ex-felon and inserting qualified criminal justice-impacted individual,
in subparagraph (J), by striking the period at the end and inserting , or, and
by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:

subsection.text: The amendments made by subsections (a), (b), and (c) shall apply to individuals who begin work for the employer after the date of the enactment of this Act.

paragraph.header: Regulations by Secretary of the Treasury
paragraph.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall prescribe such regulations or other guidance as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of the amendments made by subsections (a), (b), and (c), including by implementing the recommendations described in subclauses (I) and (II) of paragraph (2)(B)(i).

subparagraph.text: The Comptroller General of the United States shall conduct a study on the efficiency of the administrative process through which employers may claim the credit determined under section 51(a) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986.

Not later than 1 year after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Comptroller General shall submit to Congress and the Secretary of the Treasury a report on the study described in subparagraph (A), and such report shall include—

clause.text: recommendations for enhancing the efficiency of the administrative process referred to in such subparagraph, including by—

subclause.text: improving interagency coordination and data collection procedures for purposes of carrying out such administrative process, and
consolidating and simplifying any informational requirements on employers claiming the credit determined under section 51(a) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, and

clause.text: such other information as the Comptroller General determines appropriate.
